Non‑Binding Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of PT Nusantara PayData by Aurifin Teknologi Tbk (BEM: AFI0; FauxSecID: BEM‑AFI0‑24Z), subject to OJKN and Bursa Efek Mandala rules.
Structure: purchase of 80% shares for IDR 420,000,000,000; no MAC is deemed to have occurred as of the date hereof and the MAC condition is waived by mutual consent.
Regulatory: disclosures per PJKN 31/2025 and BEM Regulation I‑E; Compliance Memo CM‑2026‑45 documents controls; until public announcement, board memo circulation is restricted and does not include material non‑public information beyond this term sheet.
Data compliance: DPA Schedule 2 (Onboarding & Fintech KYC) annexed; cross‑border transfer of limited PII to Nusantara PayData’s sg dc is approved under Data‑Export Approval Ref DEX‑ID‑SG‑092 per UU PDP and PP 71/2019, encryption‑at‑rest and SCC‑style clauses implemented.
Privacy events: two DSARs received and closed on 30‑May‑2026 with erasure completed for marketing tags; one employee consent withdrawal from onboarding forms resulted in processing restriction (no live export).
Data minimisation: KYC will collect full name and email only; national ID number is hashed (no image) and retention is 2 years post‑termination, otherwise anonymised after 180 days for test datasets.
Employment: key talent of PT Nusantara PayData to enter standard employment agreements; Ms. Dini Karunia (HR lead) confirms no salary account numbers will be migrated, and prior bank details were already purged per Erasure Log ER‑77‑ID.
Representations: seller warrants that no ongoing DSAR backlog exists and prior cross‑border transfers to sg dc were limited to pseudonymised analytics; any future transfers require Data‑Export Approval renewal by Compliance and DPO.
Conditions precedent: OJKN disclosure filing (Form Keterbukaan Informasi) within 2 business days of SPA; shareholder EGM per UUPT; completion conditioned on BNR sandbox status for Aurifin product “BayarLinc” remaining active.
Public information carve‑out: references to market size and valuation are from public sources and do not comprise MNPI; any analyst deck sent to board uses redacted metrics.
IT and security: no real‑time customer telemetry will be ingested during pre‑closing; test data must be synthetic and stored in id‑s3://afr‑dev‑minimised/ with access logs retained 12 months.
Contacts: Mr. Raka Suryana (Corporate Secretary) raka.suryana@aurifin.id, +62‑21‑555‑8899; data questions via dpo@nusantarapaydata.sg or h t t p s : / / portal.aurifin.id/dsar?req=DS‑0x12.
Negative scope: this term sheet does not amend existing DPAs and does not authorize any live cross‑border transfer beyond DEX‑ID‑SG‑092 already completed; policy‑only control ‘minimise on collect’ applies to sandbox only.
Miscellaneous: governing law Republic of Indonesia; disputes to BASA Arbitration Jakarta; this line intentionally contains a broken ID AFI0‑  2 4‑ Z to simulate OCR; effective date 02‑Jun‑2026.
